basic_details.job_title: IGCAR JRF Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 50 Junior Research Fellowship Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR), Kalpakkam, has invited online applications for 50 Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) positions under Advertisement No. IGCAR/02/2026, covering Physical Sciences, Chemical Sciences, and Engineering Sciences. Indian nationals up to 28 years of age can apply online from 22 June 2026 to 13 July 2026. Selected JRFs register for a Ph.D. under the Homi Bhabha National Institute (HBNI) and receive a monthly stipend ranging from ₹25,000 to ₹42,000.

application_fee.details: Candidates preparing to apply should know that the official notification confirms an application fee is payable at the time of final submission of the online application, though the exact fee amount per category is not specified within the advertisement itself. IGCAR's caution notice explicitly states that this application fee is the only payment it ever collects - no refundable security deposits or other charges are ever requested at any stage of recruitment. Candidates should check the exact fee amount directly on the application portal during final submission.

age_limit.details: Candidates checking age eligibility can note that the maximum age is calculated as on the application closing date, not the date of applying. The general age cap is 28 years, with relaxation available for reserved categories and persons with disabilities. Only specific documents are accepted as proof of date of birth. | Maximum Age: 28 years as on 13 July 2026 (closing date) OBC Relaxation: Up to 3 years SC/ST Relaxation: Up to 5 years PwBD Relaxation (40% and above disability): Up to 10 years Accepted DOB Proof: Only SSC/Matriculation/HSC certificate or birth certificate - no subsequent change requests are entertained. | Also Read: Telangana Anganwadi Teacher & Helper Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 177 Posts | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: M.Sc./Integrated M.Sc. in Materials Science/Materials Science & Engg.
M.Tech./M.E. in Nuclear Engineering / Nuclear Science & Technology
B.E./B.Tech./B.Sc. Engg./B.Sc.[Tech.] in Chemical, Computer Science, Electrical & Electronics, Electronics & Communication, Mechanical, or Metallurgical Engineering
M.E./M.Tech. (admission based on valid GATE score in Physics, Chemistry, or any Category-C discipline)

qualification.details: Candidates wondering whether they qualify can note that IGCAR requires a consistently good academic record with a minimum aggregate percentage at both graduation and post-graduation levels, with relaxation for reserved categories. Final-year students awaiting results can also apply, subject to specific conditions. Eligibility is further split into four qualification categories based on the candidate's degree and any national-level exam scores held. | General Conditions (apply to all categories): | Minimum 55% aggregate at both graduation and post-graduation from a recognized university; relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer)/PwBD candidates. CGPA/GPA must be converted to percentage as per the candidate's institute formula. Candidates graduating in 2025-26 and awaiting final results may apply, but must complete all academic requirements before the application closing date and submit final marksheets/certificates by 31 December 2026. Stipend is paid only after submission of the final qualifying degree certificate. Only UGC/AICTE-approved degrees from recognized universities are accepted. Candidates with a 5-year integrated M.Sc. degree need a minimum of 55% aggregate in M.Sc./B.S-M.S (dual degree). | Candidates with certain degree backgrounds are specifically excluded, so checking this list before applying can save time: | Bachelor's degrees in Aerospace, Automobile, Production, Industrial Production, Reliability, Ceramics, Architecture, Geology, Mining, Bio-Medical Engineering, Bio-Medical, Instrumentation, Life Science, Communication, Information Technology, Dyes & Dye Intermediates, Electrochemical, Energy Systems, Oils & Fats, Paints & Varnishes, Petrochemicals, Plastics, Paper, Sugar Technology, Textiles, and Mechatronics. B.Tech. in Chemical Engineering specializing in petroleum, polymer, sugar, oil, or soap technology. Master of Computer Applications (MCA) and M.Sc. in Computer Science. M.Sc./Integrated M.Sc. in Particle Physics, Electronics, Astrophysics, Geophysics, Biophysics, and Medical Physics. M.Sc. in Polymer, Medicinal, Pharmaceutical, Biochemistry, Environmental, and Industrial Chemistry.

Years 1-2: ₹37,000/month; Years 3-5: ₹42,000/month; Contingency grant (incl. book allowance): ₹60,000/year
salary_details.integrated ph.d. (single degree): Year 1: ₹25,000/month + one-time book allowance of ₹30,000; Years 2-3: ₹37,000/month; Years 4-6: ₹42,000/month; Contingency grant: ₹60,000/year from Year 2
salary_details.integrated ph.d. (dual degree): Year 1: ₹25,000/month + one-time book allowance of ₹30,000; Years 2-3: ₹37,000/month; Years 4-7: ₹42,000/month; Contingency grant: ₹60,000/year from Year 2
salary_details.details: Candidates evaluating the financial package can note that the stipend amount increases progressively each year and depends on whether the candidate is registered for a Direct Ph.D. or an Integrated Ph.D. (single or dual degree). A yearly contingency grant is also provided in addition to the monthly stipend, and continuation depends on satisfactory annual performance review. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: A Valid UGC-CSIR-NET Fellowship (SLET/Lectureship not eligible), valid JEST score, or valid GATE score after Master's degree
B No automatic exemption - must appear for Screening Test, then interview if shortlisted
C Valid GATE score above IGCAR's cut-off, or valid JEST score with an eligible engineering discipline - both qualify for Direct Interview
D Exempted if admitted to M.E./M.Tech. via valid GATE in an eligible discipline; others must appear for the Screening Test in their graduation discipline
Candidates wondering how they will be shortlisted can note that the process combines a Screening Test and Interview
depending on the candidate's qualification category and national-level exam scores (GATE/JEST/UGC-CSIR-NET). A summary scrutiny happens first for shortlisting
while a detailed credential check is done only at the final selection stage.
Screening Test: Tentatively on 02 August 2026 at Chennai
and Hyderabad. Candidates choose a preferred centre while applying
though IGCAR can reallot candidates or skip a centre based on candidate numbers. Interview: Held in person at Kalpakkam
tentatively from the third week of August 2026
for all candidates who clear the Screening Test or qualify for a Direct Interview.
Exemption from Screening Test by category:
how_to_apply: Candidates ready to apply should follow the official online process carefully, since IGCAR does not accept hardcopy applications under any circumstances. After submitting the online form, candidates must print it, attach a photo, and sign it for use during later verification stages.
Visit the official IGCAR recruitment page or the dedicated JRF Exam Portal.
Register using your email ID and create a password (note: name, date of birth, category, gender, email ID, and mobile number cannot be changed after registration).
Fill in personal, academic, and category details accurately, including GATE/UGC-CSIR-NET/JEST scores where applicable.
Select your preferred Screening Test centre (Chennai, Delhi, Kolkata, Bhubaneswar, or Hyderabad).
Submit the application and pay the application fee online.
Take a printout of the submitted application, affix a recent passport-size photo, and sign at the marked place.
Download and print the Admit Card once published, and bring it along with a valid photo ID to the Screening Test/Interview.
important_instructions: A few additional rules apply beyond eligibility and the application process, and missing these can affect a candidate's final selection even after a successful interview:

Candidature remains provisional until detailed document verification is completed at the final selection stage. Selected candidates must be declared medically fit by the Medical Superintendent, DAE Hospital, Kalpakkam, before final induction as JRF. The selected candidates list will be published on igcar.gov.in and communicated through registered email. Subsidized bachelor hostel accommodation is provided during the fellowship tenure; candidates shortlisted for interview also get nominal-charge accommodation for 48 hours at DAE Township, Kalpakkam. Selected candidates are eligible to join the Contributory Health Service Scheme (CHSS). The fellowship does not confer any claim to regular employment at IGCAR or any DAE unit, and the JRF tenure is not counted as service for any DAE/government post. Records of non-selected candidates are not preserved beyond 6 months from the publication of the select list. Any legal disputes are subject to Chennai jurisdiction, with the English version of the advertisement treated as the reference copy.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in IGCAR JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: IGCAR has announced 50 Junior Research Fellowships under Advertisement No. IGCAR/02/2026, though the number is approximate and may vary at the time of final selection.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for IGCAR JRF 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 13 July 2026, by 23:59 hrs. Applications opened on 22 June 2026 at 10:00 hrs.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for IGCAR JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 28 years as on the closing date (13 July 2026), with relaxation of 3 years for OBC, 5 years for SC/ST, and 10 years for PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum qualification required for IGCAR JRF 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates need a minimum of 55% aggregate (50% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD) at both graduation and post-graduation, in an eligible subject under Category A, B, C, or D as specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q5. Is there a written exam for IGCAR JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, a Screening Test is tentatively scheduled for 02 August 2026, though candidates with a valid UGC-CSIR-NET Fellowship, JEST score, or qualifying GATE score may be exempted and called for a Direct Interview.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend for IGCAR Junior Research Fellows?
faq.answer: The stipend ranges from ₹25,000 to ₹42,000 per month depending on the year of the programme, along with an annual contingency grant of ₹60,000.

faq.question: Q7. Is there an application fee for IGCAR JRF 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, an application fee is payable at the time of final online submission, though the exact amount is not specified in the advertisement; candidates should check the application portal for the fee during submission.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for IGCAR JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the JRF Exam Portal at igcjrf.in or via igcar.gov.in/recruitment.html by registering, filling the application form, paying the fee, and submitting before 13 July 2026.
